concept: Sample / Selected Portion or Observation for Examination
description: A selected portion or observation taken for examination. How well a sample stands for something larger rests entirely on how it was selected, never on how precisely it was measured once taken.
sound_symbolism: The example-like opening 'phisu' marks a selected case, while final 'ri' gives that selection a traceable path into examination. The movement stops before inference, refusing to promise that the sample represents its larger source.
grammatical_notes: A countable noun commonly modified by source or material: 'mia ha phialu phisuri masue.' (I measure this water sample.) Preserve exact site, identifier, time, method, and chain-of-custody details in a separate source record. Distinct from 'phisuwa' (example), 'thesori' (evidence), population, and a complete body of material.

pillars.secular-buddhist-philosophy: A sample makes partial access explicit and resists turning one observed portion into certainty about a larger whole.
pillars.peace-linguistics-practices: Selection, custody, permission, and generalization remain visible instead of being hidden behind the authority of a scientific-sounding object.

tags.cognition: a selected portion whose relation to a larger source must be justified
tags.physical: a material, organism, place, or record portion taken for examination
